data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

The chunk under review is the docs-only plan "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern" (with the Pass-4 coach as a documented topic). The T3 overlay fires only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a previously-unused capability — neither happens here.

Grounded rationale:
- Scope is explicit: "Docs only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d." The What/AC describe authoring documentation (gate behavior, three-pass mechanism, config surface incl. the kill-switch, fail-open, cert semantics, CI-check pattern, the Pass-4 affirmative coach) and CROSS-REFERENCING internal artifacts — 6f2d (WS-DOC) and 75a9. Cross-references to internal rebar tickets are, per the overlay's ANTI-FP clause, an already-used / internal integration → not-applicable.
- (a) technical feasibility: documenting an already-decided/built system is achievable as described; no capability gap. The load-bearing architecture decisions live in sibling/parent tickets (6f2d → e6d9; the coach design in 75a9), which this plan documents rather than newly integrates.
- (b) named subcommands/endpoints: none are named — there is no external CLI/API surface to verify MATCH/MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED against --help/docs.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions: not implicated — no external service is contacted by a docs deliverable.
- (d) capability-gap → SPIKE: no asserted-but-unverified external capability is depended upon, so no SPIKE routing is warranted.

The plan's own text affirmatively renders every T3 sub-check satisfied or not-applicable; a well-specified docs plan with no external integration is a clean PASS.

Affirmations (T3): integration achievable as described (no external integration; internal cross-references only); no MISMATCH/UNVERIFIED external subcommand/endpoint risk; no missing auth/HTTPS precondition; no critical capability gap requiring a SPIKE.
